heading: Public and private laws, postal conventions, and treaties
label: 709 Public and private laws, postal conventions, and treaties
text: The Director of the Government Publishing Office shall print in slip form copies of public and private laws, postal conventions, and treaties, to be charged to the congressional allotment for printing and binding. The Joint Committee on Printing shall control the number and distribution of copies.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on 44 U.S. Code, 1964 ed., § 191 (, ; , ).Jan. 12, 1895, ch. 23, § 5628 Stat. 609July 10, 1952, ch. 632, § 766 Stat. 541
Amendments2014— substituted “Director of the Government Publishing Office” for “Public Printer”.Pub. L. 113–235
